{"results":[{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Decision: Use asyncio.Semaphore for concurrency limiting in run_tasks

The run_tasks function caps concurrency via asyncio.Semaphore(max_concurrent). Each task wrapper does `async with sem:` before running the actual callable, so at most `max_concurrent` wrappers hold the semaphore at once. This is simpler and more idiomatic than manual counting or a work-queue pattern.

Why: asyncio.Semaphore integrates cleanly with cancellation — on CancelledError, the `async with sem.__aexit__` releases the semaphore automatically. A manual counter would require a try/finally in every wrapper, which is error-prone. Semaphore also handles the fairness question implicitly (FIFO acquisition order in CPython).","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_cancel_async_tasks_run_0da12edb","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["DEC","USE","SEM","THE","EAC","THI","WHY","CAN","FIF","CPY"],"field":"general","flags":["CORE"],"importance_stars":2,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"run","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.20196282863616943,"source_file":"session: implementing run_tasks in run.py","topics":["decision","asyncio","semaphore","concurrency","limiting","in","run","tasks","function","caps","via","max","concurrent","each","task","wrapper","does","async","sem","before","running","actual","callable","so","at","most","wrappers","hold","once","is","simpler","more","idiomatic","than","manual","counting","or","a","work","queue","pattern","integrates","cleanly","cancellation","on","cancellederror","aexit","releases","automatically","counter","would","require","try","finally","every","which","error","prone","also","handles","fairness","question","implicitly","fifo","acquisition","order","cpython"],"wing":"cancel-async-tasks"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Decision: Validate max_concurrent >= 1 with explicit ValueError

If max_concurrent < 1, raise ValueError immediately before creating any tasks.

Why: asyncio.Semaphore(0) would deadlock — no task could ever acquire it. asyncio.Semaphore with a negative value raises ValueError from the Semaphore constructor, but validating before any infrastructure is set up makes the contract explicit and the error clearer to callers.","domain":"project","drawer_id":"drawer_cancel_async_tasks_run_1627e5a0","emotions":["determ"],"entities":["DEC","VAL","DJC","WHY","SEM"],"field":"general","flags":["TECHNICAL"],"importance_stars":3,"memory_kind":"evidence","room":"run","route":{"confidence":0,"reason":"global search: no taxonomy keyword match","room":null,"wing":null},"similarity":-0.2048511505126953,"source_file":"session: implementing run_tasks in run.py","topics":["decision","validate","max","concurrent","1","explicit","valueerror","if","raise","immediately","before","creating","any","tasks","asyncio","semaphore","0","would","deadlock","no","task","could","ever","acquire","it","a","negative","value","raises","from","constructor","but","validating","infrastructure","is","set","up","makes","contract","error","clearer","to","callers"],"wing":"cancel-async-tasks"},{"anchor_id":"repo://legacy","anchor_kind":"repo","content":"Decision: Use try/except BaseException around asyncio.gather for cancellation handling

The outer try/except catches any BaseException (CancelledError, task exceptions) from the gather, then: (1) cancels all not-done runner tasks to prevent queued-but-not-started tasks from beginning execution, (2) awaits asyncio.wait(runners) to let in-flight tasks run their cleanup code, and (3) re-raises the original exception.

Why: asyncio.gather propagates cancellation to its children but does not wait for their cleanup before raising. The explicit cancel-then-wait sequence ensures that CancelledError reaches each started runner, their try/finally or async-with cleanup executes, and the semaphore is released before we re-raise.
